headline: Child Malnutrition Persists at 21% Among B40 Children Under 5
context: UNICEF-MOH survey found that 1 in 5 B40 children under 5 shows signs of stunting or wasting.

cards.big: 21% of B40 children under 5 show stunting or wasting in 2025 UNICEF survey
cards.sub: Malaysia's childhood malnutrition rate rivals some low-income countries

cards.big: National stunting rate of 14.2% exceeds the 10% threshold indicating a public health problem
cards.sub: Stunting causes irreversible cognitive and physical development damage

cards.big: KEMAS nutrition program reaches 320,000 children but 180,000 eligible children are excluded
cards.sub: Coverage gaps concentrate in urban poor communities and Orang Asli settlements

cards.big: Peru reduced childhood stunting from 28% to 12% through integrated nutrition and cash transfers
cards.sub: Multi-sectoral approach addressing poverty alongside nutrition proved most effective

cards.big: Children who are stunted at 5 carry that disadvantage for life—early intervention is the only window
Should Malaysia declare childhood malnutrition a national emergency and fund universal nutrition programs
